scope_note: QA Fibonacci Inert Frobenius Conjugation cert [424]. Primary sources: Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' doi:10.2307/2309169 (alpha(p)|p-(5/p); inert case gives alpha(p)|p+1); Lagrange, J.-L. (1771) 'Demonstration d'un theoreme d'arithmetique' Nouveaux Memoires de l'Academie de Berlin pp. 125-173 (order divides group size; applied to F_{p^2}× of order p^2-1=(p-1)(p+1)); Chebotarev, N. (1926) 'Die Bestimmung der Dichtigkeit einer Menge von Primzahlen welche zu einer gegebenen Substitutionsklasse gehoeren' Mathematische Annalen 95 pp. 191-228 (Frobenius equidistribution; inert primes correspond to non-trivial Frobenius in Gal(Q(sqrt5)/Q)=Z/2Z). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): For inert primes p ((5/p)=-1, i.e. p%5 in {2,3}): (A) x^2-x-1 is irreducible over F_p; (B) the Frobenius automorphism Frob_p: x->x^p in F_{p^2}/F_p sends phi_tilde |-> psi_tilde (swaps the two roots); (C) alpha(p) | p+1; (D) ord_{F_{p^2}×}(phi_tilde/psi_tilde) = alpha(p). F_{p^2} SETUP: F_{p^2} = (Z/pZ)[x]/(x^2-x-1) [irreducible mod p for inert p]; elements: (a,b) for a+b*phi; multiplication: (a,b)*(c,d) = (ac+bd, ad+bc+bd) mod p [using phi^2=phi+1]; Frobenius: Frob(a+b*phi) = a+b*phi^p = a+b*psi = (a+b)+(-b)*phi = ((a+b)%p, (-b)%p) [since phi^p=psi by Frobenius in F_{p^2}/F_p]; norm: N(a+b*phi) = a^2+ab-b^2 [= (a+b*phi)(a+b*psi) = a^2+ab*(phi+psi)+b^2*phi*psi = a^2+ab*1+b^2*(-1)]. FROBENIUS CONJUGATION PROOF: For inert p, phi satisfies x^2-x-1=0 over F_{p^2} but not F_p. The Frobenius sigma: x->x^p is the unique non-trivial automorphism of F_{p^2}/F_p. Since phi is a root of x^2-x-1=0 (defined over F_p), sigma(phi)=phi^p is the OTHER root = psi. Direct: phi=(0,1); phi^p computed in F_{p^2} by fast exponentiation; expected = psi=(1,p-1). ALPHA DIVIDES P+1: (phi/psi)^p = phi^p/psi^p = psi/phi = (phi/psi)^{-1} [using Frob swaps phi<->psi]; therefore (phi/psi)^{p+1} = (phi/psi)^p*(phi/psi) = (phi/psi)^{-1}*(phi/psi) = 1 in F_{p^2}×; so ord(phi/psi) | p+1; and since alpha(p)=ord(phi/psi) [inert analogue of [423] C3], alpha(p)|p+1. INERT ORDER IDENTITY: alpha(p) = ord_{F_{p^2}×}(phi/psi). Proof: F_n=0 mod p iff phi^n=psi^n in F_{p^2} iff (phi/psi)^n=1 in F_{p^2}× [same argument as split case but now phi,psi in F_{p^2}×]; so alpha(p) = ord_{F_{p^2}×}(phi/psi). CONTRAST WITH [423]: split p: phi,psi in F_p×; Frob=identity; alpha(p)|p-1; ord in GL_1(F_p). Inert p: phi,psi in F_{p^2}× not F_p×; Frob swaps; alpha(p)|p+1; ord in GL_1(F_{p^2}). UNIFIED STATEMENT: alpha(p) = ord_{GL_1(F_{p^{1+e_p}})}(phi/psi) where e_p=0 (split) or e_p=1 (inert); this is the GL_1/Q(sqrt 5) Langlands statement for all non-5 primes. CHECKS: (C1) IRREDUCIBILITY: x^2-x-1 has no roots in F_p for all 47 inert primes <=500; falsifiable: any inert p with a root in F_p refutes; (C2) FROBENIUS CONJUGATION: phi^p=(1,p-1)=psi in F_{p^2} for 12 inert primes <=100; direct fp2_pow(0,1,p,p) computation; falsifiable: any p where phi^p != psi refutes; (C3) ALPHA DIVIDES P+1: alpha(p)|p+1 for all 47/47 inert primes <=500; falsifiable: any p where alpha(p) does not divide p+1 refutes; (C4) INERT ORDER IDENTITY: ord_{F_{p^2}×}(phi/psi) = alpha(p) for all 47/47 inert primes <=500; falsifiable: any inert prime where the F_{p^2}× order disagrees with alpha(p) refutes. LANGLANDS CHAIN: [423] alpha(p)=ord_{GL_1(F_p)}(phi/psi) for split p -> [424] Frob_p: phi<->psi in F_{p^2}/F_p for inert p; alpha(p)|p+1; alpha(p)=ord_{GL_1(F_{p^2})}(phi/psi). Together [423]+[424] complete the GL_1/Q(sqrt 5) Frobenius order story for all non-5 primes. All integer arithmetic (F_{p^2} elements are pairs of integers mod p); no float; Theorem NT. Derived 2026-06-14.
